MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anna Harris, Esq. 925-734-0990 of Clapp Moroney for Cross Complainant/Cross Defendant 5 Star Flooring.
James Bohm, Esq. 714-384-6500 of Bohm Wildish & Matsen for Defendant/Cross Complainant SBS Corp.
Jeremy Beal, Esq. 714-578-4050 of Bullard Brown & Beal LLP for Cross Defendant Allied Heating and Air Conditioning through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Atty. Harris argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT FIVE STAR FLOORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Five Star Floors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication as to SBS Corporation's cross-complaint is denied. Five Star failed to maintain its initial burden of production. (See Code Civ. Proc. 437c(p)(2).) The lynchpin of Five Star's motion is that it was performing its work in the kitchen area and the accident occurred at a different portion of the worksite. Five Star relies on 184:4-7 of plaintiff's deposition to show that it was working in the kitchen. (Five Star's UMF 15.) Five Star mischaracterizes plaintiff's testimony. Plaintiff merely testified that Five Star started bringing flooring material to the kitchen. The record is thus unclear precisely where Five Star was performing its services. The court sustains SBS's objection to the declaration of Eric Ross as lacking foundation. (See Code Civ. Proc. 437c(d) ["Supporting and opposing affidavits or declarations shall be made by a person on personal knowledge, shall set forth admissible evidence, and shall show affirmatively that the affiant is competent to testify to the matters stated in the affidavits or declarations."]; Evid. Code 702(a) ["the testimony of a witness concerning a particular matter is inadmissible unless he has personal knowledge of the matter."]; Mamou v. Trendwest Resorts, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 686, 692 fn.1 ["bald recital" that contents of declaration are based on declarant's personal knowledge cannot "satisfy the proponent's burden to affirmatively demonstrate that the witness is testifying from his own perception of the events he describes."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
